name: oi_contraction_bull
intuition: When open interest contracts during a bullish trend, it suggests weak hands are exiting and the trend is sustainable. This contrasts with funding-driven trades because it captures position unwinding rather than cost-of-carry dynamics.
anti_correlation: Uses OI decline instead of negative funding; uncorrelated with funding z-score signals.

name: taker_buy_surge_breakout
intuition: A surge in taker buy volume relative to sell volume indicates aggressive market buying, often preceding or confirming a breakout. This captures order-flow imbalance, a different dimension than funding rates.
anti_correlation: Focuses on aggressive buying pressure rather than funding cost; secondary vol_surge confirms participation.

name: fear_buy_with_trend
intuition: Extreme fear (low FGI) often marks capitulation, but buying into fear is only profitable when the long-term trend remains intact. This sentiment-driven entry is orthogonal to funding-based signals.
anti_correlation: Uses sentiment index (FGI) instead of funding; requires bull200 to avoid catching falling knives.

name: liquidation_flush_reversal
intuition: When liquidations are high relative to open interest, overleveraged positions have been flushed, reducing selling pressure. A reversal long in a bullish trend exploits this mean-reversion, distinct from funding-based edges.
anti_correlation: Uses liquidation metrics rather than funding; targets post-flush bounces.

name: volume_exhaustion_short
intuition: Extreme volume spikes (vol_ratio >> 1) after a prolonged uptrend can signal climax buying and exhaustion. Shorting with a bearish trend filter captures reversals, unlike funding-based longs.
anti_correlation: Short-side hypothesis using volume exhaustion; secondary bull50 (short-term trend) ensures entry after a recent high.

name: defi_leverage_contraction_long
intuition: Low DeFi utilization indicates reduced borrowing demand, often coinciding with deleveraging bottoms. When utilization is low and price is above EMA200, a long captures the subsequent re-leveraging cycle.
anti_correlation: Uses DeFi lending utilization, a macro leverage proxy; only applies to ETH and SOL (major DeFi chains).

name: crosssec_funding_divergence_long
intuition: When an asset's funding rate is significantly lower than the cross-sectional mean, it is relatively cheap to hold long. Combined with a bullish trend, this captures relative value without relying on absolute funding levels.
anti_correlation: Uses relative funding across assets rather than absolute funding z; proven insight suggests it's uncorrelated with existing edges.

name: oi_surge_trend_continuation
intuition: A surge in open interest during an uptrend confirms new money entering the market, supporting trend continuation. This OI-based signal differs from funding-based edges by measuring commitment rather than cost.
anti_correlation: Applies to altcoins (AVAX, DOGE, ADA) where funding alpha is absent; uses OI surge instead of funding.
